heading: Evaluation of programs and projects
label: 5056 Evaluation of programs and projects

children.heading: General objectives; persons conducting the evaluation
children.label: a General objectives; persons conducting the evaluation
children.text: The Director shall measure and evaluate the impact of all programs authorized by this chapter, their effectiveness in achieving stated goals, in general, and in relation to their cost, their impact on related programs, and their structure and mechanism for delivery of services. Each program shall be evaluated at least once every three years. Evaluations shall be conducted by persons not immediately involved in the administration of the program or project evaluated. Such evaluation shall also measure and evaluate compliance with the equitable distribution requirement of .section 5054 of this title

children.heading: General standards; publication; reports of ensuing actions
children.label: b General standards; publication; reports of ensuing actions
children.text: The Director shall develop and publish general standards for evaluation of program and project effectiveness in achieving the objectives of this chapter. Reports submitted pursuant to section 5047 of this title shall describe the actions taken as a result of evaluations carried out under this section.1See References in Text note below.1

children.heading: Opinions of participants
children.label: c Opinions of participants
children.text: In carrying out evaluations under this subchapter, the Director shall, whenever possible, arrange to obtain the opinions of program and project participants about the strengths and weaknesses of such programs and projects.

children.heading: Summaries of results; publication
children.label: d Summaries of results; publication
children.text: The Director shall publish summaries of the results of evaluations of program and project impact and effectiveness no later than sixty days after the completion thereof.

children.heading: Federal property
children.label: e Federal property
children.text: The Director shall take the necessary action to assure that all studies, evaluations, proposals, and data produced or developed with Federal funds shall become the property of the United States.

children.heading: Evaluation of programs that relate to services that assist families caring for frail and disabled adult family members; evaluation of impact by volunteers on such programs; report to committees of Congress
children.label: f Evaluation of programs that relate to services that assist families caring for frail and disabled adult family members; evaluation of impact by volunteers on such programs; report to committees of Congress
A
children.text: the range and extent of service needs of, and the services provided to, family caregivers assisted by volunteers;

children.label: B
children.text: the characteristics of volunteers and the skills, training, and supervision necessary to provide various types of volunteer assistance to family caregivers;

children.label: C
children.text: administrative costs, including recruitment, training, and supervision costs, associated with volunteer assistance to family caregivers; and

children.label: D
children.text: such other issues as may be relevant to provide services to assist family caregivers;

children.label: A
children.text: information on adminstrative costs associated with such volunteers;2So in original. Probably should be “administrative”.2

children.label: B
children.text: a comparison of the quality of services provided by such volunteers and the quality of services provided by volunteers who receive a stipend under such parts, including the rate of absenteeism and turnover; and

children.label: C
children.text: a review of the effect that participation by volunteers who do not receive such stipend have on the administration of such programs; and
submit to the authorizing committees a report summarizing in detail the results of the evaluations made under paragraphs (1) and (2).

children.heading: Funds limitation; reduction of allotments
children.label: g Funds limitation; reduction of allotments
children.text: The Director is authorized to use such sums as are required, but not to exceed 1 per centum of the funds appropriated under this chapter, to conduct program and project evaluations (directly, or by grants or contracts) as required by this chapter. In the case of allotments from such an appropriation, the amount available for such allotments (and the amount deemed appropriate therefor) shall be reduced accordingly.
